steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the passage. Every sentence describes Nora's internal reactions — her fear, her disinterest, her shock and puzzlement — so the purpose must be about portraying Nora's perspective toward Clara's friends.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "To portray Nora's reaction to Clara's friends": The passage details exactly this: Nora was afraid of them, found their topics uninteresting, and was shocked and puzzled. Every sentence supports a focus on Nora's feelings. ✓
  • "To identify the topics Nora talks about with Clara's friends": The passage says Clara's friends "talked about things of which she knew nothing" but never names those topics. No specific topics are identified. ✗
  • "To explain how Clara met some of her friends": The passage never mentions how Clara met her friends. This information is entirely absent. ✗
  • "To illustrate how Clara's friends feel about Nora": The passage focuses on Nora's feelings, not the friends' feelings about Nora. The friends' perspective is never given. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
